_comment: Ralph Wiggum — Claude Code Hooks Template
Copy relevant hooks to your project's .claude/settings.json
These enforce golden rules automatically — the agent can't forget them
PostToolUse hooks run AFTER every tool call of the specified type
If the hook exits non-zero, Claude sees the error and auto-fixes
This turns 'please do X' into 'you must do X'
Customize the commands for your project's linter/formatter

PostToolUse._when: After any file is written or edited — catches style violations immediately
After any file is written or edited — auto-formats
After any TS/TSX file is written — catches type errors early

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx)$'; then npx tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -20; fi

PostToolUse._when: For projects using Biome instead of ESLint/Prettier
After any Python file is written

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '\.py$'; then ruff check --fix "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null && ruff format "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; fi || true

PostToolUse._when: After any Go file is written

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '\.go$'; then gofmt -w "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; fi || true

PreToolUse._when: Prevents force pushes, hard resets, and branch deletions

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_BASH_COMMAND" | grep -qE '(push.*--force|reset.*--hard|branch.*-D|clean.*-f)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Dangerous git operation. Ask the user first.' >&2; exit 1; fi

PreToolUse._when: Prevents accidentally staging everything (secrets, build artifacts, etc.)

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_BASH_COMMAND" | grep -qE 'git add (-A|\.)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Use git add with specific files, not -A or dot.' >&2; exit 1; fi

PreToolUse._when: Prevents committing files that likely contain secrets

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_BASH_COMMAND" | grep -qE 'git add.*(\.(env|pem|key)|credentials|secret)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Potential secret file detected. Review before committing.' >&2; exit 1; fi
